Comparison · 2026

contextprompt vs Krisp
for engineering teams

Krisp is the gold standard for meeting noise cancellation. But if your meetings produce code, the right tool goes further than clean audio.

Try contextprompt free Free plan · No credit card required
TL;DR

Krisp excels at what it was built for: industry-leading noise cancellation at the OS audio level, plus basic AI meeting summaries. If audio quality is a problem, Krisp solves it. contextprompt is purpose-built for engineering teams: it scans your repos, maps every coding decision to real file paths, and outputs structured tasks formatted for Claude Code, Cursor, or any AI coding assistant. If your standup should produce Jira tickets instead of paragraphs, contextprompt is the tool.

Feature comparison

What Krisp and contextprompt offer for software development teams.

Feature contextprompt Krisp
Meeting transcription ✓ Zoom, Meet, Slack, Teams ✓ 16 languages
AI meeting summary ✓ Included ✓ Included
Speaker identification ✓ With attribution ✓ Included
Noise cancellation – Not available ✓ Industry-leading
Works with any conferencing app Via bot (URL-based) ✓ OS audio level
Repo / codebase scanning ✓ File paths + exports ✗ Not available
Tasks with real file paths ✓ Mapped to your code ✗ Not available
Implementation steps per task ✓ Per task ✗ Not available
AI coding assistant output ✓ Paste into Claude Code ✗ Not available
GitHub issue analysis ✓ Pro feature ✗ Not available
AI chat about meetings – Not available ✓ Included
Desktop app required ✗ No install needed ✓ Windows / Mac
Free tier 1 hr recording / month 60 min noise cancel/day, 2 summaries
Pro pricing $30 / mo (account) $16 / user / mo
Developer-focused output ✓ Built for engineers General meeting notes

Where contextprompt pulls ahead

Krisp cleans your audio. contextprompt turns your meeting into something you can actually ship.

📁

Repo-aware task extraction

contextprompt scans your connected repos before analyzing the transcript. When your team mentions "refactor the user service" or "fix the price formatting bug," contextprompt resolves those to real file paths — like src/services/user.ts and src/utils/format.ts.

🤖

Output formatted for AI coding agents

Every task contextprompt produces includes implementation steps, file paths, and the exact transcript quote that triggered it — ready to paste into Claude Code, Cursor, or any AI coding assistant. Krisp gives you a paragraph summary. You still have to figure out what to build and where.

🚀

No install, no setup

contextprompt uses a meeting bot — you paste the URL, the bot joins. No desktop app, no browser extension, no audio driver configuration. Krisp requires a Windows or Mac app to apply its noise filter at the OS audio level.

🔇

Krisp wins: noise cancellation

Krisp's noise suppression technology is genuinely industry-leading. If you work in a noisy environment — open office, coffee shop, home with kids — Krisp's real-time audio cleaning makes your calls significantly better for everyone on the call. contextprompt has no equivalent feature.

🌐

Krisp wins: multilingual transcription

Krisp transcribes in 16 languages. contextprompt's task extraction is optimized for English. If your engineering team operates in multiple languages, Krisp handles that more reliably.

💰

contextprompt wins: team pricing

contextprompt Pro is $30/month for the whole account. Krisp Pro is $16/user/month — a 3-person team pays $48/month on Krisp vs $30/month on contextprompt, and gets far richer coding task output in return.

Who should pick which

These tools solve different problems — the right one depends on what you need from your meetings.

Choose contextprompt if you…

You need actionable code output

  • Meetings should produce tasks, not paragraphs
  • Use Claude Code, Cursor, or Copilot to implement features
  • Need tasks that reference real files, not "the auth thing"
  • Run engineering standups, sprint planning, or design reviews
  • Have a team of 2+ (per-account pricing scales well)
  • Don't want to install anything on your computer
Choose Krisp if you…

You need clean audio

  • Work in a noisy environment where background noise is an issue
  • Need transcription in a non-English language
  • Want noise cancellation that works with any conferencing app
  • Prefer a lightweight desktop app over a browser-based tool
  • Need basic meeting notes without repo-level coding tasks

Common questions

What engineering teams ask when comparing Krisp and contextprompt.

Can Krisp generate coding tasks from meetings?
Krisp generates generic meeting summaries and basic action items, but it has no awareness of your codebase. It cannot produce tasks with real file paths, implementation steps, or confidence levels. For that level of structured developer output, contextprompt is designed specifically for engineering teams.
Does contextprompt have noise cancellation like Krisp?
No. contextprompt does not include noise cancellation. Krisp's noise suppression is genuinely best-in-class and operates at the OS audio level. If background noise is a problem on your calls, Krisp handles that well — and the two tools operate at different layers, so you could use both simultaneously.
What is the main difference between Krisp and contextprompt for developers?
Krisp processes audio on your device — it suppresses noise and generates a basic transcript and summary. contextprompt sends a meeting bot, receives the transcript, scans your connected repos, and extracts structured coding tasks with real file paths and implementation steps tailored for AI coding assistants like Claude Code or Cursor.
How does contextprompt work without OS-level audio access?
contextprompt uses a Recall.ai bot that joins your meeting as a participant via the URL. The bot receives the audio and transcribes it server-side. No browser extension, no desktop app, no OS audio driver setup — paste the meeting link and the bot does the rest.
Is contextprompt cheaper than Krisp for a team?
For a solo developer, Krisp Pro ($16/mo) is cheaper than contextprompt Pro ($30/mo account). For a team of two or more, contextprompt Pro is equal or cheaper — and delivers substantially richer developer output with repo-aware task extraction. Krisp Business is $30/user/month, which makes contextprompt dramatically cheaper for any team of two or more.

Clean audio is the start. Shipping code is the goal.

contextprompt joins your call, scans your repos, and extracts structured coding tasks with real file paths — ready for Claude Code. Free plan, no credit card, no install.
